terms.definition: A map of the sky at the exact moment of your birth, showing the positions of all planets, the Sun, and the Moon relative to the zodiac signs and houses. It serves as your cosmic blueprint.
The zodiac sign rising on the eastern horizon at the moment of birth. It represents your outward personality, first impressions, and how you approach new beginnings.
The highest point in the natal chart, representing your public image, career path, and life direction.
The point directly opposite the Ascendant, marking the cusp of the 7th house. It describes the qualities you seek in partnerships.
The lowest point of the chart, opposite the Midheaven. It represents your roots, home life, inner foundation, and psychological base.
The zodiac sign the Sun occupied at your birth. It represents your core identity, ego, vitality, and the essence of who you are becoming.
The zodiac sign the Moon occupied at birth. It governs your emotional nature, instincts, subconscious patterns, and what you need to feel secure.
The planet of communication, intellect, and mental processes. Mercury governs how you think, learn, speak, and process information.
The planet of love, beauty, values, and pleasure. Venus shows what you find attractive, how you express affection, and your relationship to aesthetics.
The planet of action, desire, energy, and assertion. Mars reveals how you pursue goals, express anger, and channel physical vitality.
The planet of expansion, wisdom, abundance, and good fortune. Jupiter shows where you experience growth and philosophical understanding.
The planet of structure, discipline, responsibility, and time. Saturn represents life lessons, boundaries, and mastery through perseverance.
The planet of revolution, innovation, and sudden change. Uranus disrupts the status quo and brings flashes of insight and originality.
The planet of dreams, intuition, spirituality, and transcendence. Neptune dissolves boundaries and connects you to creative imagination.
The planet of transformation, death and rebirth, power, and deep psychological processes. Pluto reveals where profound change occurs.
A mathematical point representing your soul's evolutionary direction — the qualities and growth edges you are meant to develop in this lifetime.
Opposite the North Node, representing past-life gifts and familiar patterns you may need to move beyond for growth.
The first sign (March 21 – April 19). A cardinal fire sign ruled by Mars. Embodies initiative, courage, independence, and pioneering spirit.
The second sign (April 20 – May 20). A fixed earth sign ruled by Venus. Represents stability, sensuality, patience, and material security.
The third sign (May 21 – June 20). A mutable air sign ruled by Mercury. Embodies curiosity, communication, adaptability, and versatility.
The fourth sign (June 21 – July 22). A cardinal water sign ruled by the Moon. Represents nurturing, emotional depth, and protective instincts.
The fifth sign (July 23 – August 22). A fixed fire sign ruled by the Sun. Embodies creativity, self-expression, generosity, and confidence.
The sixth sign (August 23 – September 22). A mutable earth sign ruled by Mercury. Represents analysis, service, health, and refined craftsmanship.
The seventh sign (September 23 – October 22). A cardinal air sign ruled by Venus. Embodies harmony, partnership, justice, and aesthetic sensibility.
The eighth sign (October 23 – November 21). A fixed water sign ruled by Pluto and Mars. Represents transformation, intensity, and regenerative power.
The ninth sign (November 22 – December 21). A mutable fire sign ruled by Jupiter. Embodies adventure, philosophy, optimism, and truth-seeking.
The tenth sign (December 22 – January 19). A cardinal earth sign ruled by Saturn. Represents ambition, discipline, and lasting achievement.
The eleventh sign (January 20 – February 18). A fixed air sign ruled by Uranus. Embodies innovation, humanitarianism, and visionary thinking.
The twelfth sign (February 19 – March 20). A mutable water sign ruled by Neptune. Represents intuition, compassion, imagination, and spiritual connection.
Twelve divisions of the birth chart representing different areas of life, from identity (1st) to the unconscious (12th).
The House of Self. Governs identity, physical appearance, first impressions, and how you initiate action.
The House of Partnership. Governs committed relationships, marriage, and business partnerships.
The House of Career. Governs public reputation, career achievements, authority, and life direction.
The House of Home. Governs family, roots, emotional foundation, and private life.
An aspect where two planets are at the same degree (0° apart). Conjunctions blend and amplify planetary energies into a unified force.
An aspect where two planets are 180° apart. Creates tension between polarities, demanding balance and integration.
An aspect where two planets are 120° apart. Represents harmony, natural talent, and easy flow between energies.
An aspect where two planets are 90° apart. Creates friction and challenge that drives action, growth, and breakthroughs.
An aspect where two planets are 60° apart. Offers opportunities and supportive connections that require conscious effort to activate.
The current movement of planets through the zodiac as they form aspects to your natal chart, activating different life areas.
A predictive technique where each day after birth symbolically represents one year. Reveals internal evolution and unfolding potential.
A period when Mercury appears to move backward. Associated with communication mishaps and revisiting past matters. A time for review.
When a planet appears to move backward from Earth's perspective. Retrograde periods internalize a planet's energy, prompting reflection.
When Saturn returns to its natal position (every ~29.5 years). A major rite of passage marking restructuring and responsibility.
The moment the Sun returns to its exact natal position each year. The solar return chart reveals themes for the year ahead.
The eight phases of the lunar cycle from New Moon through Full Moon and back. Each phase carries distinct energy for intention and release.
Powerful lunations near the lunar nodes. Solar eclipses catalyze new beginnings; Lunar eclipses bring culminations. Eclipses accelerate change.
The comparison of two natal charts to understand relationship dynamics, revealing attraction, compatibility, and growth potential.
A chart created from the midpoints of two people's natal planets. Represents the relationship itself as its own entity.
Choosing the most auspicious time for important endeavors based on planetary alignments.
A branch answering specific questions by casting a chart for the moment the question is asked.
One of four classical elements. Fire signs (Aries, Leo, Sagittarius) embody passion, enthusiasm, courage, and creative self-expression.
One of four classical elements. Earth signs (Taurus, Virgo, Capricorn) embody practicality, stability, and material mastery.
One of four classical elements. Air signs (Gemini, Libra, Aquarius) represent intellect, communication, and social connection.
One of four classical elements. Water signs (Cancer, Scorpio, Pisces) embody emotion, intuition, and deep psychological awareness.
